headline: Federal-state water dispute leaves Kedah with RM800 million owed
context: Kedah claims the federal government owes RM800 million in raw water compensation dating back to 2005 for water supplied to Penang and Perak through the Muda River system.

cards.big: Kedah says federal government owes RM800 million for 21 years of water supply
cards.sub: Inter-state water compensation dispute has dragged on since 2005 without resolution

cards.big: Water supply falls under concurrent list — both federal and state have jurisdiction
cards.sub: Constitutional ambiguity creates disputes that neither level of government resolves

cards.big: Kedah is Malaysia's 3rd poorest state with GDP per capita of RM28,400
cards.sub: RM800 million owed represents nearly 8% of Kedah's entire annual budget

cards.big: National Water Services Commission formed in 2008 but has no arbitration power
cards.sub: SPAN can regulate operators but cannot resolve federal-state financial disputes

cards.big: Water doesn't respect state borders — but Malaysia's governance framework pretends it does
Should a federal water tribunal be created to settle inter-state resource disputes?
